prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for furan; headline value reported = largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ on atom 5.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with default RI density fitting (PySCF backend via chemkit).
• Geometry provenance: fixed-geometry single-point on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz (no re-optimization; DFT//input-XYZ).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software/backend: chemkit invoking PySCF (version as on engine), via fukui-reactivity skill.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged; integrity.status = “ok”, integrity.trustworthy = true. All warnings reproduced verbatim below; no convergence failures.
• Live log: calculation was streamed to the .out file announced at launch (path available on request) for real-time monitoring.

Key numerical results (units: e):
– Most electrophilic site: atom 5 (C) with f⁺ = 1.9242931.
– Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C) with f⁻ = 0.1616371.
– Dual descriptor Δf (atom 5) = 1.762656.

All atomic f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and Δf arrays, Mulliken charges for neutral/anion/cation, result JSON, and PNG plot are saved at the paths provided by the engine.

Verbatim warnings from chemkit:
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
